html:
EmptyOccupiedSleepingHungryStarvingDeadSkeleton

The Birdcage is a craftable Structure that allows the domestication of Birds (Redbirds, Crows, Snowbirds, Canaries, etc.) It requires 6 Gold Nuggets, 2 Papyrus, and 2 Seeds to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.

A Birdcage can only house one bird; however, it can be easily swapped with another at any time. There is no functional difference between the different types of birds, other than the aesthetic appeal, with the exception of Canaries in the Caves when Toadstool is alive, and Lunar variants like Misshapen Birds, Moonblind Crows and Bright-Beaked Birds. Birds go to sleep at Night and wake up in the morning. During night, the bird can be taken out and put back in to interact with it for a short time, before it falls asleep again.

Birds will slowly starve in the Birdcage, lasting for roughly 20 Days before dying, depending on ambient temperature. Once the bird has died, the cage must be mucked out before a new bird is caged. Similar to dying in the inventory or a chest, a bird dying in a Birdcage will be changed into a Morsel, which will be fresh at the moment the bird dies, and will spoil at its normal spoiling rate. Leaving the dead bird in the birdcage will eventually turn it into a skeleton, mucking out which will yield one Rot. Unlike starving in the inventory or being killed, a caged bird will never drop a Feather.

Examining the Birdcage when the captured bird is hungry or starving will make the character say different Quotes, and the hungry bird will have an additional animation, allowing the player to know when the bird needs to be fed without removing the bird. Any item the bird can eat will restore full hunger value to the bird.

Contents

  • 1 Usage
  • 2 Tips
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Skins
  • 5 Assets
  • 6 Gallery

Usage

A bird in a Birdcage can be fed uncooked Seeds, uncooked Crop Seeds, uncooked Farm Plant crops, or most Meat-based foods (including Dishes), excluding any Eggs and Switcherdoodles, and will produce fresh seeds or Eggs.

Feeding Seeds or Crop Seeds only has a 33% chance to drop Guano, without dropping any kind of seeds.

Feeding a Farm Plant yields one item of its corresponding Crop Seed.

Feeding any meat food yields an Egg.

Misshapen Birds and Moonblind Crows can only produce Rotten Eggs or Rot.

Bright-Beaked Birds can only produce Pure Brilliance when fed 3 Infused Moon Shards.

Tips

  • Spoiling Meat foods can be saved from turning into Rot or Rotten Eggs by converting them into fresh Eggs.
  • The bird can be used to convert some type of meats that are not accepted by the Pig King (like Monster Meats or Frog Legs) into Eggs, which are accepted in exchange for Gold Nuggets.
  • The Birdcage is good for Winter, when food resources are limited, as the player can feed the bird easy-to-acquire Monster Meat-items to get fresh Eggs in return, which can be eaten or further be used in Crock Pot recipes.
    • In a situation where the player is playing as Webber and does not have the ingredients for Bacon and Eggs or Pierogi, this would not be a good decision.
  • If the player is starving and there's no other food available, then the player can take the bird out of the cage to cook it for a quick Morsel.

Trivia

  • Initially, only Crows were worth keeping in birdcages because Redbirds would die in Winter and Snowbirds would die in Summer. This was changed soon after.
  • When Reign of Giants was released, caged birds needed to be fed to keep them alive just like if they were kept in the inventory. Because there was no "fuel" system to keep the birds fed for a few days, players had to manually feed them every day or return to an empty cage. Rather than add a feed tube, Klei removed the feeding requirement for caged birds.
  • The image of a bird in a gilded cage as a metaphor for being trapped in a luxury prison was first popularised in the song "A Bird in a Gilded Cage", published in 1900.
  • Even though the Guano spawned by feeding Seeds in Don't Starve Together is much smaller than the usual one, the icon is still the same. However, the size returns to normal when the server is restarted. Laying a piece of the small Guano into a Chest will not change the size, but stacking small ones with big ones will.
  • Imprisoning a Parrot Pirate will result in a normal parrot. This means that Pirate Parrots are impossible to keep around for sanity.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Woven - Distinguished Baroque Birdcage skin was introduced during the official Year of the Carrat event in 2020.
  • The Heirloom Distinguished Macabre Birdcage skin was only obtainable during official Hallowed Nights events until 2020.
  • The Distinguished Ornamental Birdcage skin is only obtainable during the official Winter's Feast event.
  • The Woven - Distinguished Romantic Birdcage skin was introduced during the official Year of the Varg event in 2018.
  • The Loyal Pirate Birdcage skin is awarded for playing the Don't Starve: Shipwrecked DLC.
  • The Loyal The Amazing Sideshow skin was awarded for watching 6 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from September 27, 2018 to October 11, 2018 and from January 17, 2019 to January 24, 2019. It is also available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei points.

Gallery

  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Burnt Birdcage (unused)
  • Redbird imprisoned in Birdcage.
  • Crow imprisoned in a Birdcage.
  • Snowbird imprisoned in a Birdcage.
  • "Imprison" option when holding a bird over an empty Birdcage.
  • Giving a Redbird Seeds.
  • A sleeping Snowbird in a Birdcage.
  • A Crow with several eggs.
  • An easter egg in vanilla Don't Starve where placing a bird in a birdcage may result in it having a small pirate hat.
  • A recently deceased Crow in Don't Starve Together.
  • The skeleton of an unknown bird in Don't Starve Together.
  • "Muck Out Birdcage" option when holding a mouse over a Birdcage with a deceased bird inside in Don't Starve Together.
  • Birdcage as seen in the poster for the Naughty and Nice update.
  • Baroque Birdcage
  • Macabre Birdcage
  • Pirate Birdcage
  • Romantic Birdcage
  • Ornamental Birdcage

“Now it just needs a bird.”
–Wilson
“Bird prison!”
–Willow
“Is home for birdies.”
–Wolfgang
“Few of us can see our cages.”
–Wendy
“I WILL IMPRISON THE FLESHLINGS”
–WX-78
“This will safely contain one avian specimen.”
–Wickerbottom
“Where I try to make birds reform their evil ways.”
–Woodie
“You'll never get me behind bars again.”
–Maxwell
“A höme för my ravens!”
–Wigfrid
“It's the jail.”
–Webber
“Suitable lodgings for a feathered beast.”
–Warly
“Needs a friend”
–Wormwood
“That's some proper metalwork.”
–Winona
“A place where little birds are penned.”
–Wortox
“Stick bird inside!”
–Wurt
“Now we just need to catch a bird!”
–Walter
“All it needs now is a little cuckoo bird.”
–Wanda
“Who's a good bird?”
–Wilson
“Ha! Gotcha now!”
–Willow
“Hello birdy!”
–Wolfgang
“He is happier now.”
–Wendy
“HA HA. THERE IS NO ESCAPE”
–WX-78
“He is contained.”
–Wickerbottom
“Think aboot what you've done, bird!”
–Woodie
“I feel for ya, feathers.”
–Maxwell
“Are yöu having a nice time?”
–Wigfrid
“Jailbird.”
–Webber
“I now have an egg farm!”
–Warly
“Tweet! Tweet!”
–Wormwood
“She was just a patsy.”
–Winona
“Hello my singing, feathered friend.”
–Wortox
“Now bird stay forever!”
–Wurt
“Aw, you miss the trees don't you?”
–Walter
“Not a cuckoo, but close enough.”
–Wanda
“Awwww, he's asleep.”
–Wilson
“Stupid bird. Wake up!”
–Willow
“I should be quiet!”
–Wolfgang
“Dead? No. Just sleeping.”
–Wendy
“WAKE UP, FLESHLING”
–WX-78
“Shhhhh!”
–Wickerbottom
“They look so innocent when they dream.”
–Woodie
“You'll still be in there when you wake up.”
–Maxwell
“Sweet dreams, raven friend.”
–Wigfrid
“You have to sleep to survive in the jail.”
–Webber
“Sleep now, lay later.”
–Warly
“Sleep well, Tweeter”
–Wormwood
“Why are you tired? Your life is so cushy.”
–Winona
“No more sleepless nights you'll spend.”
–Wortox
“Nighty night, birdy.”
–Wurt
“Are you sleeping?”
–Walter
“You could be spending this time laying eggs!”
–Wanda
“He's looking a bit peckish.”
–Wilson
“What's all the fuss about?”
–Willow
“I hear tiny grumbles!”
–Wolfgang
“Must be hungry.”
–Wendy
“FLESHLINGS ARE SO PICKY”
–WX-78
“He's getting hungry.”
–Wickerbottom
“I'll get you something to eat soon, buddy.”
–Woodie
“What's all the fuss about, feathers?”
–Maxwell
“What dö yöu want, little friend?”
–Wigfrid
“He looks hungry.”
–Webber
“Let me cook something nice up for you.”
–Warly
“Hungry?”
–Wormwood
“Hey! Is it my turn to feed the bird?”
–Winona
“No worries, lunch is right around the bend.”
–Wortox
“Bird needs snack.”
–Wurt
“We need some bird food!”
–Walter
“Is it feeding time?”
–Wanda
“Has no one fed you in awhile?”
–Wilson
“He won't be quiet!”
–Willow
“His tiny stomach is empty!”
–Wolfgang
“The emptiness is eating him from the inside out.”
–Wendy
“WHAT? WHAT DO YOU WANT?”
–WX-78
“He's looking a bit malnourished.”
–Wickerbottom
“He's got a hankering for some poutine!”
–Woodie
“Oh, come on. I fed you last week.”
–Maxwell
“The raven wishes tö feast!”
–Wigfrid
“We need to feed him.”
–Webber
“Oh, what do birds eat? A nice brisket?”
–Warly
“Needs food!”
–Wormwood
“This poor bird's a bag of bones.”
–Winona
“Does no one have some seeds to lend?”
–Wortox
“It look so hungry...”
–Wurt
“Oh no! Did we forget to feed you?”
–Walter
“Oh botheration, I forgot to feed you again, didn't I?”
–Wanda
“Maybe he's just resting?”
–Wilson, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“At least he's quiet now.”
–Willow, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“Birdy? Are you okay?”
–Wolfgang, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“Bereft of life, he rests in pieces.”
–Wendy, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“IT'S BROKEN”
–WX-78, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“I do believe he has starved.”
–Wickerbottom, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“I'm sorry. I failed you.”
–Woodie, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“That's a deep sleep.”
–Maxwell, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“Yöu can feast in Valhalla nöw, friend.”
–Wigfrid, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“We weren't very good wardens.”
–Webber, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“Maybe it will wake up.”
–Warly, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“Oh. Whoops”
–Wormwood, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“I think it was my turn to feed her.”
–Winona, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“It seems you went and met your end.”
–Wortox, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“Aw, it dead now.”
–Wurt, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“I'm... a terrible pet owner...”
–Walter, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“It was... probably just his time.”
–Wanda, when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary
“That bird is definitely deceased.”
–Wilson
“That should probably be cleaned up.”
–Willow
“He is not okay.”
–Wolfgang
“That... is an ex-bird.”
–Wendy
“IT MUST BE OUT OF BATTERIES”
–WX-78
“I guess I could study the bones.”
–Wickerbottom
“That's starting to smell.”
–Woodie
“Maybe he wasn't sleeping.”
–Maxwell
“The cage is söiled.”
–Wigfrid
“Eww.”
–Webber
“It is not waking up. Oh dear.”
–Warly
“(sigh)”
–Wormwood
“Let's uh... just sweep that under the rug.”
–Winona
“I don't think she's exactly on the mend.”
–Wortox
“...”
–Wurt
“Someone should bury it.”
–Walter
“Oh botheration, I forgot all about him!”
–Wanda
The Birdcage is a craftable Structure that allows the domestication of Birds (Redbirds, Crows, Snowbirds, Canaries, etc.) It requires 6 Gold Nuggets, 2 Papyrus, and 2 Seeds to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.
A Birdcage can only house one bird; however, it can be easily swapped with another at any time. There is no functional difference between the different types of birds, other than the aesthetic appeal, with the exception of Canaries in the Caves when Toadstool is alive, and Lunar variants like Misshapen Birds, Moonblind Crows and Bright-Beaked Birds. Birds go to sleep at Night and wake up in the morning. During night, the bird can be taken out and put back in to interact with it for a short time, before it falls asleep again.
Birds will slowly starve in the Birdcage, lasting for roughly 20 Days before dying, depending on ambient temperature. Once the bird has died, the cage must be mucked out before a new bird is caged. Similar to dying in the inventory or a chest, a bird dying in a Birdcage will be changed into a Morsel, which will be fresh at the moment the bird dies, and will spoil at its normal spoiling rate. Leaving the dead bird in the birdcage will eventually turn it into a skeleton, mucking out which will yield one Rot. Unlike starving in the inventory or being killed, a caged bird will never drop a Feather.
Examining the Birdcage when the captured bird is hungry or starving will make the character say different Quotes, and the hungry bird will have an additional animation, allowing the player to know when the bird needs to be fed without removing the bird. Any item the bird can eat will restore full hunger value to the bird.
Contents

2 Tips
3 Trivia
4 Skins
5 Assets
6 Gallery
Usage[edit | edit source]
A bird in a Birdcage can be fed uncooked Seeds, uncooked Crop Seeds, uncooked Farm Plant crops, or most Meat-based foods (including Dishes), excluding any Eggs and Switcherdoodles, and will produce fresh seeds or Eggs.
Feeding Seeds or Crop Seeds only has a 33% chance to drop Guano, without dropping any kind of seeds.
Feeding a Farm Plant yields one item of its corresponding Crop Seed.
Feeding any meat food yields an Egg.
Misshapen Birds and Moonblind Crows can only produce Rotten Eggs or Rot.
Bright-Beaked Birds can only produce Pure Brilliance when fed 3 Infused Moon Shards.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Spoiling Meat foods can be saved from turning into Rot or Rotten Eggs by converting them into fresh Eggs.
The bird can be used to convert some type of meats that are not accepted by the Pig King (like Monster Meats or Frog Legs) into Eggs, which are accepted in exchange for Gold Nuggets.
The Birdcage is good for Winter, when food resources are limited, as the player can feed the bird easy-to-acquire Monster Meat-items to get fresh Eggs in return, which can be eaten or further be used in Crock Pot recipes.
In a situation where the player is playing as Webber and does not have the ingredients for Bacon and Eggs or Pierogi, this would not be a good decision.
If the player is starving and there's no other food available, then the player can take the bird out of the cage to cook it for a quick Morsel.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Initially, only Crows were worth keeping in birdcages because Redbirds would die in Winter and Snowbirds would die in Summer. This was changed soon after.
When Reign of Giants was released, caged birds needed to be fed to keep them alive just like if they were kept in the inventory. Because there was no "fuel" system to keep the birds fed for a few days, players had to manually feed them every day or return to an empty cage. Rather than add a feed tube, Klei removed the feeding requirement for caged birds.
The image of a bird in a gilded cage as a metaphor for being trapped in a luxury prison was first popularised in the song "A Bird in a Gilded Cage", published in 1900.
Even though the Guano spawned by feeding Seeds in Don't Starve Together is much smaller than the usual one, the icon is still the same. However, the size returns to normal when the server is restarted. Laying a piece of the small Guano into a Chest will not change the size, but stacking small ones with big ones will.
Imprisoning a Parrot Pirate will result in a normal parrot. This means that Pirate Parrots are impossible to keep around for sanity.
Skins[edit | edit source]
Main article: Belongings
The Woven - Distinguished Baroque Birdcage skin was introduced during the official Year of the Carrat event in 2020.
The Heirloom Distinguished Macabre Birdcage skin was only obtainable during official Hallowed Nights events until 2020.
The Distinguished Ornamental Birdcage skin is only obtainable during the official Winter's Feast event.
The Woven - Distinguished Romantic Birdcage skin was introduced during the official Year of the Varg event in 2018.
The Loyal Pirate Birdcage skin is awarded for playing the Don't Starve: Shipwrecked DLC.
The Loyal The Amazing Sideshow skin was awarded for watching 6 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from September 27, 2018 to October 11, 2018 and from January 17, 2019 to January 24, 2019. It is also available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei points.

Is it my turn to feed the bird? |warly = Let me cook something nice up for you. |wortox = No worries, lunch is right around the bend. |wormwood = Hungry? |wurt = Bird needs snack. |walter = We need some bird food! |wanda = Is it feeding time? |choose = webber }} |-|Starving= {{Quotes |wilson = Has no one fed you in awhile? |willow = He won't be quiet! |wolfgang = His tiny stomach is empty! |wendy = The emptiness is eating him from the inside out. |wx78 = WHAT? Whoops |wurt = Aw, it dead now. |walter = I'm... a terrible pet owner... |wanda = It was... probably just his time. |choose = webber |source = when the bird is dead or a poisoned Canary }} |-|Skeleton= {{Quotes |wilson = That bird is definitely deceased. |willow = That should probably be cleaned up. |wolfgang = He is not okay. |wendy = That... is an ex-bird. |wx78 = IT MUST BE OUT OF BATTERIES |wickerbottom = I guess I could study the bones. |woodie = That's starting to smell. |maxwell = Maybe he wasn't sleeping. |wigfrid = The cage is söiled. |webber = Eww. |winona = Let's uh... just sweep that under the rug. |warly = It is not waking up. Oh dear. |wortox = I don't think she's exactly on the mend. |wormwood = (sigh) |wurt = ... |walter = Someone should bury it. |wanda = Oh botheration, I forgot all about him! |choose = webber }} The '''Birdcage '''is a craftable [[Structure]] that allows the domestication of [[Birds]] ([[Redbird]]s, [[Crow]]s, [[Snowbird]]s, [[Canary|Canaries]], etc.) It requires 6 [[Gold Nuggets]], 2 [[Papyrus]], and 2 [[Seeds]] to craft and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. A ''Birdcage'' can only house one bird; however, it can be easily swapped with another at any time. There is no functional difference between the different types of birds, other than the aesthetic appeal, with the exception of [[Canary|Canaries]] in the [[Caves]] when [[Toadstool]] is alive, and Lunar variants like [[Misshapen Bird]]s, [[Moonblind Crow]]s and [[Bright-Beaked Bird]]s. Birds go to sleep at [[Night]] and wake up in the morning. During night, the bird can be taken out and put back in to interact with it for a short time, before it falls asleep again. Birds will slowly [[Food#Perish Times|starve]] in the ''Birdcage'', lasting for roughly 20 [[Day]]s before dying, depending on ambient temperature. Once the bird has died, the cage must be mucked out before a new bird is caged. Similar to dying in the inventory or a chest, a bird dying in a ''Birdcage'' will be changed into a [[Morsel]], which will be fresh at the moment the bird dies, and will spoil at its normal spoiling rate. Leaving the dead bird in the ''birdcage'' will eventually turn it into a skeleton, mucking out which will yield one [[Rot]]. Unlike starving in the inventory or being killed, a caged bird will never drop a [[Feather]]. Examining the ''Birdcage'' when the captured bird is hungry or starving will make the character say different [[Quotes]], and the hungry bird will have an additional animation, allowing the player to know when the bird needs to be fed without removing the bird. Any item the bird can eat will restore full hunger value to the bird. == {{Pic32|Icon Tools}} Usage == A bird in a Birdcage can be fed uncooked [[Seeds/DST|Seeds]], uncooked [[Crop Seeds/DST|Crop Seeds]], uncooked [[Farm Plant]] crops, or most [[Food Type/DST#Meat|Meat-based foods]] (including [[Dishes]]), excluding [[Food Value/DST#Egg value|any Eggs]] and [[Switcherdoodle]]s, and will produce fresh seeds or [[Egg/DST|Eggs]]. Feeding Seeds or Crop Seeds only has a 33% chance to drop [[Guano]], without dropping any kind of seeds. Feeding a Farm Plant yields one item of its corresponding Crop Seed. Feeding any meat food yields an [[Egg]]. [[Misshapen Bird]]s and [[Moonblind Crow]]s can only produce [[Rotten Egg]]s or [[Rot]]. [[Bright-Beaked Bird]]s can only produce [[Pure Brilliance]] when fed 3 [[Infused Moon Shard]]s. == {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * Spoiling Meat foods can be saved from turning into [[Rot]] or [[Rotten Egg]]s by converting them into fresh Eggs. * The bird can be used to convert some type of meats that are not accepted by the [[Pig King]] (like [[Monster Meat/DST|Monster Meats]] or [[Frog Legs/DST|Frog Legs]]) into Eggs, which are accepted in exchange for Gold Nuggets. * The Birdcage is good for [[Winter]], when food resources are limited, as the player can feed the bird easy-to-acquire [[Monster Meat/DST|Monster Meat]]-items to get fresh Eggs in return, which can be eaten or further be used in Crock Pot recipes. ** In a situation where the player is playing as [[Webber]] and does not have the ingredients for Bacon and Eggs or Pierogi, this would not be a good decision. * If the player is starving and there's no other food available, then the player can take the bird out of the cage to cook it for a quick Morsel. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Initially, only Crows were worth keeping in birdcages because Redbirds would die in [[Winter]] and Snowbirds would die in [[Summer]]. This was changed soon after. * When ''Reign of Giants'' was released, caged birds needed to be fed to keep them alive just like if they were kept in the inventory. Because there was no "fuel" system to keep the birds fed for a few days, players had to manually feed them every day or return to an empty cage. Rather than add a feed tube, [[Klei]] removed the feeding requirement for caged birds. * The image of a bird in a gilded cage as a metaphor for being trapped in a luxury prison was first popularised in the song "[[Wikipedia:A Bird in a Gilded Cage|A Bird in a Gilded Cage]]", published in 1900. * Even though the [[Guano]] spawned by feeding Seeds in ''Don't Starve Together'' is much smaller than the usual one, the icon is still the same. However, the size returns to normal when the server is restarted. Laying a piece of the small Guano into a Chest will not change the size, but stacking small ones with big ones will. * Imprisoning a [[Parrot Pirate]] will result in a normal parrot. This means that Pirate Parrots are impossible to keep around for [[sanity]]. == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Gardening and Food Skins|Belongings}} * The '''Woven - Distinguished Baroque Birdcage''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Carrat]] event in 2020. * The '''Heirloom Distinguished Macabre Birdcage''' skin was only obtainable during official [[Hallowed Nights]] events until 2020. * The '''Distinguished Ornamental Birdcage''' skin is only obtainable during the official [[Winter's Feast]] event. * The '''Woven - Distinguished Romantic Birdcage''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Varg]] event in 2018. * The '''Loyal Pirate Birdcage''' skin is awarded for playing the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. * The '''Loyal The Amazing Sideshow''' skin was awarded for watching 6 hours of ''Don't Starve Together'' on Twitch from September 27, 2018 to October 11, 2018 and from January 17, 2019 to January 24, 2019. It is also available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei points.
